description: A double-ended queue implemented with a growable ring buffer. Supports efficient push and pop at both ends. Python equivalent: collections.deque.

methods.name: new
methods.example:
use std::collections::VecDeque;
let mut deque: VecDeque<i32> = VecDeque::new();

methods.name: push_back
methods.description: Appends an element to the back of the deque. Python equivalent: deque.append().

methods.name: push_front
methods.description: Prepends an element to the front of the deque. Python equivalent: deque.appendleft().

methods.name: pop_back
methods.description: Removes and returns the last element, or None if the deque is empty. Python equivalent: deque.pop().

methods.name: pop_front
methods.description: Removes and returns the first element, or None if the deque is empty. Python equivalent: deque.popleft().

methods.name: len
methods.description: Returns the number of elements in the deque.

methods.name: iter
methods.description: Returns a front-to-back iterator over the elements.
